content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of NNN integers, define


An array PPP of length NNN, where PiP_iPi‚Äã is the number of distinct elements in the subarray A[1:i]A[1:i]A[1:i] of AAA.
An array SSS of length NNN, where SiS_iSi‚Äã is the number of distinct elements in the subarray A[i:N]A[i:N]A[i:N] of AAA.
You are given arrays PPP and SSS. Determine whether there exists an array AAA corresponding to the given PPP and SSS arrays.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2‚Ä¶PNP_1, P_2 \ldots P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã‚Ä¶PN‚Äã.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2‚Ä¶SNS_1, S_2 \ldots S_NS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã‚Ä¶SN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output the answer on a new line: if there does not exist any array AAA producing such PPP and SSS arrays, then output `NO`, otherwise output `YES`


You can output each letter in any case.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have P=[1,2,3]P = [1, 2, 3]P=[1,2,3] and S=[3,2,1]S = [3, 2, 1]S=[3,2,1].
Consider A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3].

The number of distinct elements in the 111st prefix [1][1][1] is 111.
The number of distinct elements in the 222nd prefix [1,2][1,2][1,2] is 222.
The number of distinct elements in the 333rd prefix [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3] is 333.

and

The number of distinct elements in the 111st suffix [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3] is 333.
The number of distinct elements in the 222nd suffix [2,3][2,3][2,3] is 222.
The number of distinct elements in the 333rd suffix [3][3][3] is 111.

Clearly AAA produces the given PPP and SSS.
Test case 222: We have P=[2,2,3,3]P =[2,2,3,3]P=[2,2,3,3] , S=[3,2,2,1]S =[3,2,2,1]S=[3,2,2,1].
It can be proven that there does not exist any array AAA which produces the given PPP and SSS.
Test case 333: We have P=[1,1]P =[1,1]P=[1,1], S=[1,1]S =[1,1]S=[1,1].
Consider A=[7,7]A = [7,7]A=[7,7].

The number of distinct elements in the 111st prefix [7][7][7] is 111.
The number of distinct elements in the 222nd prefix [7,7][7,7][7,7] is 111.

and

The number of distinct elements in the 111st suffix [7,7][7,7][7,7] is 111.
The number of distinct elements in the 222nd suffix [7][7][7] is 111.

Clearly AAA produces the given PPP and SSS.
Test case 444: P=[1,2,1]P = [1,2,1]P=[1,2,1] , S=[2,2,1]S =[2,2,1]S=[2,2,1].
It can be proven that there does not exist any array AAA which produces the given PPP and SSS.
